text: Meta Reportedly Developing Prediction Markets App "Arena" to Rival Polymarket and Kalshi According to The New York Times, Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg has directed an internal team to develop a standalone prediction markets app called Arena, separate from Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp, and Messenger. The product is reportedly modeled after Polymarket and Kalshi, initially using a points-based system rather than real-money wagering, though future monetized participation has not been ruled out. Meta is said to view prediction markets as an emerging user behavior trend and a potential growth opportunity. The company previously launched a prediction app called Forecast in 2020 before shutting it down in 2022. — link

text: Ethereum Foundation Completes Organizational Restructuring, Cuts About 20% of Staff The Ethereum Foundation (EF) announced a new organizational structure after completing a months-long restructuring process. EF said the new structure will include five main work clusters covering the protocol, access, user, community and institutional layers, alongside an operations cluster and management support teams. As part of the restructuring, 54 team members will leave the foundation, representing about 20% of EF's headcount. EF said the affected employees will receive severance and transition support, and that the new structure is intended to make the organization more focused and streamlined. Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin said the Ethereum Foundation will cut its 2026 budget by about 40% as it shifts toward a long-term endowment-based model. EF aims to reduce annual spending from around 15% of remaining funds before 2026 to about 5% after 2030. Vitalik said EF will still maintain its protocol ambitions, while narrowing some workstreams, winding down PSE as a standalone unit, making Devcon smaller, and reducing institutional work. — link

text: Wintermute: Crypto Market Leverage Has Largely Been Cleared Wintermute said in its latest market update that leverage in the crypto market has largely been flushed out, while Strategy's continued Bitcoin accumulation has eased earlier concerns over potential selling pressure. However, Wintermute noted that marginal demand from major buyers such as ETFs and Strategy has weakened compared with previous phases. Until flows improve structurally, the best-case scenario may be range-bound trading rather than the start of a new uptrend. A softer U.S. PCE print or easing Middle East tensions could trigger a rebound, but Wintermute said such a move would more likely be a tactical bounce than confirmation of a market bottom. — link

text: INTEL: The Pentagon is asking Congress for $80B to cover the cost of the US war on Iran, nearly triple the $29B it estimated just last month | per AP No formal White House request yet, but Hegseth's been working the Capitol and a deputy briefed senators last week. It's running into a wall: lawmakers skeptical of Trump's Iran deal don't want to fund the war, with one Democrat saying he "hasn't found anyone who wants to do this." Sits on top of a record $1.5T Pentagon budget ask. Senators warn the real price tag could go higher. Developing. Twitter

text: INTEL: Trump signs executive orders to accelerate US quantum computing. The orders target a research-ready quantum computer by 2028, prepare the US for quantum threats to encryption, and direct billions in federal support toward the sector. A month ago the White House invested in 6 Quantum stocks: 1. IBM ($IBM) Received $1B in funding. One of the world's leading quantum computing companies, operating a large fleet of quantum systems through IBM Quantum. 2. Infleqtion Received $100M in funding. Focused on neutral-atom quantum computing, quantum sensors, atomic clocks and quantum software. 3. Rigetti ($RGTI) Received $100M in funding. Develops and operates superconducting quantum processors. 4. D-Wave ($QBTS) Received $100M in funding. A pioneer in quantum computing systems focused on solving complex optimization problems. 5. GlobalFoundries ($GFS) Received $100M in funding. Major semiconductor foundry that recently launched a dedicated quantum technology business. 6. Honeywell ($HON) Received $100M in funding. Its quantum efforts are primarily through Quantinuum, one of the leading quantum computing companies. Twitter

text: India FIU Seeks Crypto OTC Trade Records Above $10,000 From Major Exchanges India's Financial Intelligence Unit, the country's anti-money laundering watchdog, has asked three major crypto exchanges to submit information on OTC transactions above USD 10,000, with a focus on identifying the ultimate beneficial owners behind private companies, intermediaries and other entities involved. The records must be traced and retained from January 2026 onward, as regulators seek to close transparency gaps in large crypto trades conducted outside public exchange order books. — link

text: QCP Market Colour – 23 June 2026 BTC reclaimed 65k after Strategy added 520 BTC and increased cash reserves by 300m to 1.4bn, extending dividend coverage to nearly 10 months. While likely funded through its ATM equity issuance programme, investors appear to be taking comfort from the liquidity rebuild, with STRC back above 90. The broader risk picture remains mixed. Nasdaq fell around 1% as large-cap tech came under pressure, while USD/JPY moved sharply lower on renewed BoJ intervention speculation. Small caps, however, outperformed, with the Russell 2000 reaching a fresh all-time high above 3,000. For crypto, the setup is still range-bound. US-Iran uncertainty, concerns around Strategy's STRC offering, and questions over Warsh's policy approach continue to weigh on sentiment. This week's PCE inflation report and quarter-end rebalancing could drive cross-asset volatility, but crypto options markets remain unconvinced that any single catalyst is enough to force a breakout. Is BTC consolidating before quarter-end catalysts arrive, or will traders keep fading volatility until macro and crypto-specific signals align? Read the full colour here.

text: Highlight Clip CZ: Hyperliquid claims decentralization but small team retains high control CZ: Hyperliquid claims decentralization but small team retains high control On June 18, 2026, Binance founder CZ stated during an interview on the Galaxy channel that the decentralized derivatives platform Hyperliquid has validated a new market niche and shown impressive technical innovation, successfully occupying a no-KYC space that Binance cannot compete in. CZ mentioned that Hyperliquid founder Jeff participated in one of the earliest YZi Labs incubation seasons. However, CZ believes the platform is actually heavily controlled by a small team and prefers not to judge their "decentralized" claims. Discussing the historical risks of the no-KYC model, CZ expressed that given his own life experiences, he would never do what they do, but he assumes they are fully capable of handling their own affairs. — link
